reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff (Nov 9, 2025), NASA GISS data for October 2025 is not yet released (typically available mid-month). However, the anomaly for May 2025 was reported as 1.07°C, which is very close to the target range of 1.10-1.14°C. Given the broader context of 2024 being a record warm year (with October 2023 at 1.34°C and October 2024 likely high as well), and the expected transition towards La Niña in late 2025 which typically brings anomalies down slightly from record peaks, a value in the 1.10-1.14 range is a plausible outcome for the cooling trend from the 2024 highs. Historically, specific 0.05°C brackets have a relatively low base rate (approx. 10-15%), but the proximity of earlier 2025 data (1.07°C in May) suggests this bracket is in the current trend line. I've assigned a probability of 18%, reflecting it as a likely candidate but acknowledging the month-to-month volatility of climate anomalies.
